decisions.title: Canvas Architecture: Template vs. Per-Block Editor Approach
decisions.context: Two parallel Canvas initiatives (ENG-10073 template components, ENG-9957 per-block editor) suggest architectural uncertainty. Need to decide on unified approach before V2 launch to avoid technical debt.

arguments.for: Template approach enables faster iteration, consistent UX patterns, easier maintenance. Aligns with 'Nail the Core' pillar.
arguments.against: Per-block editor offers more flexibility for power users, enables complex custom workflows that differentiate from competitors (Moat building).

decisions.title: Flink 2.0 Migration Timeline for Production Workloads
decisions.context: Multiple Flink 2.0 tasks in flight (ENG-9947, ENG-10076, ENG-10077, ENG-10075). Shadow dry-run in Gov suggests cautious approach, but need commitment date to avoid prolonged dual-maintenance burden.

arguments.for: Early migration reduces technical debt, unlocks new capabilities for Edge and real-time decisioning. Supports 'Embed Deeper' with better performance.
arguments.against: Gov dry-run may surface stability issues. Premature migration risks customer-facing incidents that damage trust during critical embed phase.

decisions.title: SCIM Sync Health Visibility: MVP vs. Full Observability
decisions.context: ENG-9926 adds SCIM visibility for admins. Scope unclear—basic status vs. detailed diagnostics. Impacts enterprise embed strategy and support burden.

arguments.for: Full observability (sync logs, error details, retry status) reduces support tickets, enables self-service troubleshooting. Key for 'Embed Deeper' in enterprises.
arguments.against: MVP (green/yellow/red status) ships faster, validates demand before over-investing. Can iterate based on customer feedback aligned with Signal>Evidence>Decision.

decisions.title: Global Nav Breadcrumb Bar: Implement Now or Defer Post-IA Redesign
decisions.context: ENG-10084 introduces breadcrumb navigation. Unclear if this is foundational for larger IA work or standalone improvement. Risk of rework if IA strategy shifts.
decisions.blockedBy: Waiting on IA redesign strategy decision from product leadership. No clarity on whether breadcrumbs fit new navigation paradigm.

arguments.for: Breadcrumbs improve discoverability now, address immediate user navigation pain. Quick win for 'Nail the Core' usability.
arguments.against: May conflict with upcoming IA changes. Better to solve navigation holistically than patch incrementally.

decisions.title: Feature Flags Front-End: Build vs. Integrate Third-Party
decisions.context: ENG-9122 prototyping feature flags UI. Unclear if building proprietary system or integrating LaunchDarkly/Split. Impacts velocity and moat strategy.

arguments.for: Build: Tight integration with Sift's decision engine, becomes proprietary moat. Enables unique 'flag decisions based on live data signals' use cases.
arguments.against: Integrate: Faster time-to-value, proven UX patterns, lower maintenance. Let Sift focus on core decisioning IP, not infrastructure tooling.

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy context-switching week: 3 critical architecture decisions pending, 8 engineering streams in parallel across Canvas/Flink/Platform. Need consolidated decision forum.
alerts: Flink 2.0 migration has 4 related tickets but no single DRI—risk of coordination failure
Canvas work split across 3 engineers with no clear integration plan (ENG-10073, ENG-9957, ENG-10092)
IA/Navigation work (ENG-10084) blocked but not explicitly flagged in Linear—hidden dependency
28% of week in 1:1s—consider batching team syncs or async updates to reclaim focus time
